steps.title: Identify what the finding implies
steps.content: The researchers found that hedonic motivation positively influences the intention to adopt premium streaming. The blank asks what this finding “suggests” — so the answer must be a practical implication that follows logically from that result. If making a service feel more pleasant increases adoption intent, then companies that want more premium sign-ups should make the service feel more pleasant.

steps.title: Eliminate three choices, confirm one
steps.content:
• "it may be advantageous for music streaming companies to try to influence the extent to which potential users perceive the premium version as pleasant to use.": Directly applies the finding — boosting perceived pleasantness would raise adoption intent. ✓
  • "participants who strongly agreed with the statement "Using paid music streaming services is pleasant" were more likely to express loyalty to Apple Music than to other streaming services.": Brand-specific loyalty was never studied; this is an unsupported inference. ✗
  • "the statement "Using paid music streaming services is pleasant" is not a reliable measure of hedonic motivation.": Contradicts the study, which accepted this statement as a valid measure. ✗
  • "users' intentions to pay for premium streaming services are probably unaffected by factors other than hedonic motivation.": Overstates the result — the study showed hedonic motivation matters, not that nothing else does. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
